English: Introduction
Manx: Goan-Foslee

English: The second joint Manx Language Strategy (2022 – 2032) is a ten-year vision for effective, coordinated support for the promotion and delivery of the Manx language. This interim report relates to the second year of the strategy.
Manx: Ta’n nah Strateysh copharteeagh son y Ghaelg (2022 - 2032) ny ashlish jeih bleeaney son dy chur cooney ta fondagh as ta er ny cho-oardaghey cour cur er e toshiaght as cur ayns bree yn ghlare Vanninagh. Ta’n tuarastyl shallidagh shoh bentyn rish y nah vlein jeh’n strateysh.

English: The second year of the Manx Language Strategy (2022–2032) marks a continued commitment to promoting and delivering the Manx language effectively. Building on the foundations laid in the first year, Jeebin, the Manx Language Network, remains focused on creating a comprehensive, coordinated approach to support the language’s growth and visibility both on the Island and within the wider diaspora, working alongside the many voluntary groups that support the Manx language in diverse ways to achieve this.
Manx: She cowrey barrantys kinjagh son cur er e toshiaght as cur magh yn Ghaelg er aght fondagh ta’n nah vlein jeh’n Strateysh son y Ghaelg (2022-2032). As ad troggal er undinyn v’er nyn soiaghey ayns y chied vlein, ta Jeebin, The Manx Language Network, tannaghtyn dy chur tastey er-lheh da croo saase ooilley-ghoaillagh, er ny cho-oardaghey, ta cooney lesh yn ghlare dy aase as yn aght t’ee ry-akin chammah er yn Ellan as fud yn diaspora smoo. Ta Jeebin gobbragh marish ymmodee possanyn dy arryltee ta cummal seose yn Ghaelg ayns caghlaaghyn aght dy chooilleeney shoh.

English: Over the past year, progress has been made towards our strategic focus on raising awareness and enhancing education, with a continued emphasis on making the language accessible to all. This has involved increased efforts to provide wider access to training and the development of new resources, something that has been enhanced by the increasing use of technology for both resource development and access to training. Considerable attention has been devoted to fostering collaboration and aligning projects to develop new both visual and written resources based on identified needs, preventing duplication, and ensuring continuity. This approach has proved particularly effective in resource development across organisations. In addition, the ongoing development of the corpus enables us to explore and gain a deeper understanding of our language.
Manx: Car ny bleeaney chaie, va shin goll er nyn doshiaght dy mie lesh yn dean strateyshagh ain ta shen dy ghra dy hroggal toiggal as fys as dy vishaghey ynsagh, as tastey er-lheh er ny choyrt dy kinjagh da jannoo yn ghlare foshlit da dy chooilley pheiagh. Ghow shin ayns laue dy osley traenal da ny smoo dy ‘leih as dy chroo ny smoo couyryn, va shoh er ny vishaghey liorish ymmyd smoo dy haghnoaylleeaght son yn jees oc. Ta tastey vie er ve currit da bishaghey co-obbraghey as çheughey shaleeyn dy chroo chammah couyryn scruit noa as couyryn sooilley noa va soit er femeyn cronnit, shaghney dooblaghey nyn eabyn as shickyraghey sheerid. Ta’n saase shoh er phrowal feer vie dy chroo couyryn tessyn sheshaghtyn. Chammah’s shoh, ta lhiasaghey yn chorpus, ta goll er dy kinjagh, cur caa dooin nyn nglare y ronsaghey as toiggalys s’duiney y gheddyn jee.

English: The Isle of Man hosted a monitoring visit from the European Charter for Regional and Minority Languages Committee of Experts to assess how the Charter is applied within the Isle of Man. This visit was followed by the publication of a monitoring report in June 2024, which outlined a series of recommendations for further consideration which will actioned where possible.
Manx: Haink sleih veih Bing Oaylleeyn Conaant Coonceil yn Oarpey son Glaraghyn Ardjynagh as Myn-ghlaraghyn dys Mannin er turrys sessal v’er ny chummal ayns yn ellan dy hessal kys ta’n Conaant goll er cur ayns bree ayns Mannin. Erreish da’n sleih shoh shilley y choyrt er Mannin, va tuarystal sessal er ny choyrt magh ayns mee veanagh y touree 2024, hoilshee rolley dy haaseyn moyllit lhisagh ve er nyn resooney ny sodjey as er nyn goyrt ayns bree raad vees shen possibyl.

English: The progress detailed in this report during the second year of the Manx Language Strategy, along with the strong dedication to future progress, highlight the ongoing commitment to the development of the Manx language. This progress is further highlighted by the collective efforts spearheaded by Jeebin, in close collaboration with the broader language community. Together, these initiatives serve as a testament to the shared responsibility and determination to ensure the sustained growth and vitality of the language in the years to come
Manx: Ta’n coontey ta jeant ayns y tuarystal shoh jeh’n aght ta reddyn goll er nyn doshiaght ayns y nah vlein jeh’n Strateysh son y Ghaelg, as marish shen yn jeeanid t’er ny hoishaghey da gleashagh reddyn er nyn doshiaght ny smoo ‘sy traa ry-heet, soilshaghey magh dy vel barrantys kinjagh ayn dy lhiasaghey yn Ghaelg. Ny sodjey, ta’n speeideilys shoh jeant baghtal liorish yn aght ta sheshaghtyn er cho-obbraghey fo stiurey Jeebin, ta gobbraghey marish yn phobble glare cadjin. Cooidjagh, ta ny ventyryn shoh gymmyrkey feanish jeh’n churrym as yn slane-jeeanid ec dy chooilley pharteeas dy hickyraghey aase beayn as speeideilys y ghlare ayns y traa ry-heet.

English: Theme 1: Appropriate & accessible resources
Manx: Cooish 1: Couyryn cooie ta ry-gheddyn dy haashagh

English: Strategic Commitment
Manx: Dean Strateyshagh

English: We will encourage the development, publication and promotion of new resources.
Manx: Nee mayd greinnaghey croo, cur-magh as cur er nyn doshiaght jeh couyryn noa.

English: We will ensure any developed resources are coordinated, appropriate and of good quality, across all ages and stages.
Manx: Nee mayd shickyraghey dy bee couyryn erbee vees crooit er nyn go-oardaghey, cooie as jeh foaynoo mie, bentyn da dy chooilley eash as keim.

English: We will work to ensure greater digitisation of Manx Language resources at all levels.
Manx: Nee mayd gobbragh dy hickyraghey dy bee tooilley couyryn Gaelgagh currit ayns cummey bun-earrooagh ec dy chooilley cheim.

English: Progression
Manx: Goll er e hoshiaght

English: Mooinjer Veggey’s Resources Officer created an online Leader’s Portal on their website. This is currently only accessible by the Possan Cloie (parent and tots’ group) leaders while it is populated with new resource packs.
Manx: Ren Fer-Oik Couyryn Vooinjer Veggey croo Portal Leeideilee er-linney er yn ynnyd-eggey oc. Ta shoh foshlit ynrican da leeideilee y Phossan Cloie (possan son paarantyn as lhiennoo) choud’s ta paggaghyn couyryn goll er chur er.

English: The Resources Officer is also currently working with Possan Cloie leaders to create learning materials specifically for the parents of Possan Cloie children with vocabulary targeted at the parents of 0–4-year-olds.
Manx: Ta’n Fer-Oik Couyryn gobbragh neesht marish leeideilee Possanyn Cloie dy chroo stoo-ynsee er-lheh cour paarantyn ta nyn gloan ayns Possan Cloie, as bee yn tasht-fockle jeant cooie da paarantyn cloan ta eddyr 0 as 4 bleeaney dy eash.

English: Mooinjer Veggey has produced several new resources for primary school, preschool and parents.  They have published two series of ‘Early Reader’ chapter books, Orree and Otto, for Bunscoill Ghaelgagh.
Manx: Ta Mooinjer Veggey er chroo shiartanse dy chouyryn noa cour bunscoillyn, roïe-scoillyn as paarantyn. T’ad er chur magh daa strane dy lioaryn ‘Lhaihder Aeg’ Orree as Otto, rheynnit ayns cabdilyn, cour yn Vunscoill Ghaelgagh.

English: Bunscoill Ghaelgagh teacher Aalin Clague has written two new series of early readers about Celts and Vikings written in easily decodable text, to follow on from the Orree and Otto books commissioned and produced by Mooinjer Veggey for the school. These are yet to be published
Manx: Ta ben-ynsee y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh, Aalin Clague er screeu daa strane noa dy lioaryn lhaihder aeg mychione ny Celtiee as ny Shenn Loghlinee er nyn screeu ayns glare so-hoiggal, ta geiyrt er lioaryn Orree as Otto va er nyn oardaghey as er nyn yannoo liorish Mooinjer Veggey son y scoill. Cha nel ad shoh er ve currit magh foast.

English: Bunscoill Ghaelgagh teacher, Adrian Cain, created a new Manx course for families of Bunscoill Ghaelgagh, entitled Cre ren oo jiu? There is a textbook, accompanying digital content and weekly, in-person lessons supported by a WhatsApp group.
Manx: Ren fer-ynsee y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh, Adrian Cain, coorse Gaelgagh noa y chroo cour lughtyn-thie y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh, enmyssit Cre ren oo jiu? Ta lioar-teks ayn, cooid vun-earrooagh ta goll maree, as lessoonyn shiaghtinoil ta ayns-persoon, as possan WhatsApp dy chooney lesh studeyryn.

English: Bunscoill Ghaelgagh pupils created new films in Manx and were shortlisted for BBC Alba’s Film G competition for Yn Doour.
Manx: Chroo paitçhyn y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh filmyn noa ayns Gaelg as va Yn Doour er ny choyrt er y rolley giare son co-hirrey Film G er BBC Alba.

English: Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh (Manx Language Unit) have further developed their website with Key Stage 2 interactive resources https://manxlanguage.sch.im/. Members of the team have contributed additional digitised searchable materials to the Manx Corpus website, development of an interactive gamified course commencing with using vocabulary from Key Stage 2 to develop speaking, listening, reading, and writing skills.
Manx: Ta’n Skimmee Gaelgagh er lhiasaghey nyn ynnyd-eggey ny sodjey trooid cur couyryn eddyr-obbragh cour Ard-Cheim 2 er, https://manxlanguage.sch.im/. Ta oltyn y skimmee er chur ny smoo stoo ayns cummey bun-earrooagh oddys sleih ronsaghey er ynnyd-eggey yn Chorpus Gaelgagh.  T’ad er choo coorse eddyr-obbragh jeant ny ghamman, ta goaill toshiaght lesh tasht-fockle veih Ard-Cheim 2 dy yannoo ny share schleiyn loayrt, eaishtagh, lhaih as screeu.

English: Over the past year, the Manx Language Research Group (MLRG) continued to consolidate its activities with regard to the Manx Corpus website https://corpus.gaelg.im as well as research dissemination and outreach activities. Highlights have included organizing the Ned Maddrell Lecture at the Cooish, and being awarded a £3000 grant from Culture Vannin adding time-stamped transcriptions and translations of Manx language archive recordings to the corpus.
Manx: Car ny bleeaney chaie, ta’n Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh er hannaghtyn dy yeeraghey yn obbyr oc er ynnyd-eggey yn Chorpus Gaelgagh https://corpus.gaelg.im chammah’s skeaylley magh obbyr ronsee as cooishyn ta roshtyn magh dys y theay. Mastey ny reddyn cooilleenit dy mie va reaghey Leaght Ned Maddrell ec y Chooish, geddyn toyrtys argidoil jeh £3000 veih Culture Vannin son mac-screeunyn as çhyndaaysyn y yannoo jeh shenn recortyssyn Gaelgagh, cowraghyn-traa y choyrt orroo, as ad y chur ayns y Chorpus.

English: The Manx Corpus website has grown significantly and now contains 2,039,805 words across 750 documents, up from 1,826,332 words and 719 documents last year.
Manx: Ta ynnyd-eggey yn Chorpus Gaelgagh er n’aase dy jarroo as nish t’eh cummal 2,039,805 fockle harrish 750 docamadyn, shoh mooadaghey cosoyllit rish 1,826,332 fockle as 719 docamadyn veih nurree.

English: New developments include the addition of new archive audio material with timestamped transcriptions and translations, drawing on the materials recently catalogued and remastered via Manx National Heritage’s participation in the British Library’s ‘Unlocking Our Sound Heritage’ initiative. Priority is being given to native speaker recordings, but many of the tracks also contain material from prominent pioneering revivalists from the mid-twentieth century, including Douglas Fargher, William Radcliffe, and a teenage Brian Stowell. This work is being undertaken by Manx language expert Paul Rogers, supported by a grant of £3,000 from Culture Vannin. The first track to be completed was launched to significant interest at the Ned Maddrell Lecture at the 2023 Cooish festival.
Manx: Mastey reddyn noa ta shenn recortyssyn elley, lesh cowraghyn-traa orroo as çhyndaaysyn as mac-screeunyn jeant jeu er ve currit ayns y chorpus. T’ad shoh goit veih recortyssyn va catalogit as aavainshterit er y gherrid tra ghow Eiraght Ashoonagh Vannin ayrn ayns shalee y Lioarlan Goaldagh ‘Unlocking Our Sound Heritage’. Ta tastey er-lheh goll er cur da recortyssyn ny loayrtee dooghyssagh, agh ta mooarane jeh ny trackyn cummal stoo veih leeideilee scanshoil yn aavioghey neesht, veih mean yn ‘eedoo cheead, goaill stiagh Doolish y Karagher, Illiam y Radlagh, as Brian Stowell tra v’eh ny yeigeyr. Ta Paul Rogers, fer mie-ynsit ayns cooishyn Gaelgagh goaill ayns laue yn obbyr shoh, as cooney echey veih toyrtys argidoil jeh £3,000 voish Culture Vannin. Va’n chied track va jeant aarloo er ny lunney ec Leaght Ned Maddrell ayns feailley yn Chooish 2023 as va sleih soiaghey mooar jeh.

English: Other additions include the important 1763 edition of the New Testament Gospels and Acts, further instalments of Bishop Wilson’s sermons, as well as numerous items from twentieth-century newspapers which document the development of the language revival movement as well as the Island’s social and cultural history.
Manx: Mastey reddyn noa elley er ny choyrt da’n chorpus ta’n cur magh scanshoil voish 1763 jeh Sushtalyn as Jannooyn y Chonaant Noa, meeryn elley jeh sharmaneyn y niaspick Wilson, chammah’s ymmodee peeshyn elley veih pabyryn-naight yn ‘eedoo cheead ta myr docamad jeh aavioghey ny Gaelgey as shennaghys sheshoil as cultooroil yn Ellan myrgeddin.

English: Beyond the corpus, Dr Christopher Lewin has completed the draft of his new edition of Edmund Goodwin's First Lessons in Manx for Yn Cheshaght Ghailckagh (Manx Language Society), and it is hoped it will be published. Dr Lewin has also created a pronunciation and spelling guide with financial support from Culture Vannin, which will be released soon. Additionally, Professor Max W. Wheeler's new edition of Pargeiys Caillit (Paradise Lost) is nearing publication, also to be released by Yn Cheshaght Ghailckagh. This landmark of Manx literature was the topic of Professor Wheeler’s Ned Maddrell Lecture.
Manx: Chammah’s yn corpus, ta Dr Custal Lewin er chooilleeney dreaght yn chur magh noa echey jeh First Lessons in Manx liorish Edmund Goodwin ass lieh Yn Çheshaght Ghailckagh, as t’eh jerkit dy bee eh currit magh. Ta Dr Lewin er chroo lioar-stiuree cour fockley magh as lettraghey neesht lesh cooney argidoil veih Culture Vannin, vees currit magh dy leah. Ny sodjey, ta cur magh noa yn Olloo Max W. Wheeler jeh Pargeiys Caillit faggys da ve aarloo dy ve currit magh, liorish Yn Çheshaght Ghailckagh myrgeddin. She yn ard-teks shoh ayns lettyraght Vannin va cooish Leaght Ned Maddrell v’er ny choyrt liorish yn Olloo Wheeler.

English: Culture Vannin has created and published over 1,000 sound files on the Learn Manx website, in the ‘spoken dictionary’ area.  This incorporates digitising and editing recordings created by the late Brian Stowell to help families access and use Manx, creating 800 individual sound files which are accessible here.
Manx: Ta Culture Vannin er chroo as er chur magh ny shlee na 1,000 coadan sheean er ynnyd-eggey Ynsee Gaelg, raad ta’n ‘Fockleyr Loayrt’ ry-gheddyn. Ta shoh goaill stiagh cur ayns cummey bun-earrooagh as femblal recortyssyn chroo Brian Stowell (nagh vel er mayrn) dy chooney lesh lughtyn-thie roshtyn as jannoo ymmyd jeh Gaelg, ta shen dy ghra 800 coadan sheean er nyn groo ta ry-gheddyn ayns shoh.

English: A set of 25 ‘chatbots’ was developed and published in April 2024 to support learners on the first part of Culture Vannin’s four-part course, Loayr Gaelg. These give learners the capacity to consolidate their Manx through some simple reading and writing exercises. The chatbot can offer support and reacts in real-time to responses.
Manx: Ta set dy 25 ‘çhatbotyn’ er ny chroo as er ny chur magh ayns mee s’jerree y niarree 2024 dy chooney lesh ynseydee er y chied ayrn jeh coorse Culture Vannin ta kiare ayrn echey, Loayr Gaelg. T’ad shoh cur da ynseydee yn caa dy niartaghey yn Ghaelg oc trooid cliaghtaghyn lhaih as screeu aashagh. Foddee yn çhatbot çhebbal cooney as t’eh freggyrt feyshtyn çhelleeragh.

English: The first Culture Vannin Manx Gaelic Summer School for adults was introduced in July 2023. It took place across the course of five days and was intentionally scheduled to coincide with Yn Chruinnaght Celtic Gathering festival. Two courses took place, and 20 students attended, including eight students who had travelled to the Island to take part. The summer school is planned to be an annual event.
Manx: Va’n chied Scoill Souree Ghaelgagh ayn son sleih aasit ayns mee s’jerree yn touree 2023. V’ee queig laa er liurid as er ny cummal as ee kiarit dy ve ec yn un traa as va feailley Yn Chruinnaght goll er. Va daa choorse ayn, as va 20 studeyr ayn, goaill stiagh hoght studeyryn v’er hroailt dys yn Ellan dy ghoaill ayrn. T’eh kiarit dy bee yn Scoill Souree taghyrt dagh blein maghey shoh.

English: Jeebin now has a resources sub-committee, which has met three times. This aids Jeebin members working within education to focus on need, work collaboratively, and avoid duplication.
Manx: Ta fo-ving couyryn ec Jeebin nish, t’er veeiteil three keayrtyn. Ta shoh cooney lesh oltyn Jeebin ta gobbragh ayns cooishyn ynsee dy chur tastey er-lheh da feme, co-obbraghey, as shaghney doobley nyn eabyn.

English: Future Work
Manx: Obbyr yn Traa Ry-heet

English: Mooinjer Veggey have significant ambition to produce a lot more video, audio and written material for use in preschool, primary school and by families using Manx to raise their children. It is also intended that the recently developed online Leader’s Portal will soon be made available to the public.
Manx: Ta Mooinjer Veggey feer jeean dy chroo foddey smoo stoo ayns cummey feeshan, sheean, as scruit dy ve jeant ymmyd jeh ayns roïe-scoillyn, bunscoillyn as liorish lughtyn-thie ta jannoo ymmyd jeh’n Ghaelg as ad troggal nyn gloan. T’eh kiarit neesht dy bee yn Portal Leeideilee, va er ny chroo er y gherrid jeant foshlit da’n theay dy leah.

English: The Bunscoill Ghaelgagh intends to develop further decodable readers, including a set of animal stories written by teacher Aalin Clague inspired by Manx Fairy Tales.
Manx: Ta’n Vunscoill Ghaelgagh kiarail dy yientyn ny smoo dy lioaryn so-lhaih, nyn mast’oc strane dy skeealyn baagh er nyn screeu liorish ben-ynsee Aalin Clague hooar breeaghys veih Skeealyn Mooinjer Veggey Vannin.

English: Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh is still engaged in the development of support resources and a video-based grammar course with the involvement of young Manx speakers. Also, to be added on their website digital guided readers with animations, further development of the course vocabulary. In addition, the team are further enhancing the Manx Corpus by adding a wider variety of texts including those appropriate for younger learners and readers of Manx.
Manx: Ta’n Skimmee Gaelgagh foast lhiasaghey couyryn coonee as coorse grammar feeshanagh ta goaill stiagh loayrtee Gaelgagh aegey. Bee lioaryn lhaih stiurit lesh annymaghtyn, as tasht-fockle smoo son y choorse er nyn goyrt er yn ynnyd-eggey oc neesht. Ny sodjey, ta’n skimmee jannoo ny share jeh’n Chorpus Gaelgagh trooid cur caghlaa mooar dy heksyn huggey nyn mast’oc teksyn ta cooie da ynseydee as lhaihderyn Gaelgagh saa.

English: The Culture Vannin grant for the MLRG will fund continued work on audio archive material for the next year and a half. Individual tracks will be made available as and when they are finished.
Manx: Ver toyrtys argidoil Culture Vannin da’n Phossan Ronsee Gaelgagh argid cour tooilley obbyr bentyn rish shenn recortyssyn rish y vlein dy lieh shoh çheet. Bee trackyn ry-gheddyn tra vees ad jeant as aarloo.

English: Other materials will continue to be added from all periods of the language. Upcoming additions include the remaining text of Phillips’ Prayer Book, the earliest extended text in the language (c. 1610), and a range of more recent texts such as selected extracts from Brian Stowell’s autobiography.
Manx: Bee stooghyn elley currit hug yn Chorpus veih dy chooilley eash jeh’n ghlare. Mastey reddyn vees currit huggey dy gerrid ta cooid elley yn teks Lioar Phadjeragh Phillips, yn teks mooar s’moghey ayns y çhengey (c.1610), as caghlaa dy heksyn s’jeianee lheid as meeryn reiht ass hene-hennaghys Vrian Stowell.

English: Beyond the immediate goals, MLRG aims to explore innovative ways in which the Corpus can serve as the foundation for future resources, potentially leading to the development of new dictionaries and language tools.
Manx: Çheu-mooie jeh ny deanyn t’ad goaill ayns laue ec y traa t’ayn, ta’n PRG kiarail dy gheddyn magh aghtyn noa-chliaghtagh ayndoo oddys yn Corpus ve myr undin son couyryn ‘sy traa ry-heet, oddagh leeideil dys croo fockleyryn as greinyn-çhengey noa.

English: Theme 2: Manx as an important part of national & cultural identity.
Manx: Cooish 2: Gaelg myr ayrn scanshoil jeh enney ashoonagh as cultooroil

English: Strategic Commitment
Manx: Dean Strateyshagh

English: We will work together to ensure that Manx language continues to be visible, inclusive and accessible.
Manx: Nee mayd gobbragh cooidjagh dy yannoo shickyr dy jean yn Ghaelg tannaghtyn dy ve ry-akin mastey’n theay, foshlit da dy chooilley pheiagh as aashagh dy gheddyn.

English: We will promote Manx as a valued part of our distinctive contemporary, cultural and national identity
Manx: Nee mayd cur y Ghaelg er e toshiaght myr ayrn scanshoil jeh’n enney ashoonagh as cultooroil er-lheh as noa-emshyragh ain.

English: We will ensure that the advantages of bilingualism are recognised.
Manx: Nee mayd jannoo shickyr dy bee vondeishyn daa-hengaghys er nyn doiggal.

English: Progression
Manx: Goll er e hoshiaght

English: Mooinjer Veggey continues to frequently promote the Manx language through its Possan Cloie groups, through its nursery and through its social media.
Manx: Ta Mooinjer Veggey tannaghtyn dy chur er e toshiaght yn Ghaelg trooid nyn Bossanyn Cloie, nyn roïe-scoill as nyn meanyn sheshoil oc.

English: There has been difficulty this year recruiting a development officer to replace a previous officer. A successful recruitment process has recently taken place for an officer who will promote the use of Manx and expand the number of active supporters and use of Manx.
Manx: V’eh doillee myleeaney fer-oik-lhiasee y phointeil dy ghoaill ynnyd fer-oik v’ayn roïe. Va speeideilys ayn er y gherrid tra va fer-oik er ny phointeil vees cur er e hoshiaght ymmyd ny Gaelgey as vees mooadaghey yn earroo dy ‘leih ta pohlldal as jannoo ymmyd jeh’n Ghaelg.

English: Bunscoill Ghaelgagh pupils and teachers raised the profile of Manx by representing the language at national and international events. This included winning the Crucial Crew cup for the second year in a row with a film in Manx Gaelic (the competition is for all Year 6 classes on the Island), presenting stories written in Manx on Tynwald Day and participation and success sharing our language at Aundyryn Kiaull Theay Vannin, the Manx Folk Awards and the Manx Music Festival. Two of our pupils were proud to represent the Island in Glasgow for the FILM G awards in February.
Manx: Hrog paitçhyn as fir as mraane-ynsee y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh enney er y Ghaelg liorish shassoo ass lieh yn ghlare ec taghyrtyn ashoonagh as eddyr-ashoonagh. Ghow shoh stiagh cosney cappan yn Crucial Crew son daa vlein ass y cheilley lesh fillym ayns Gaelg (ta’n co-hirrey foshlit da dy chooilley vrastyl Blein 6 er yn Ellan), taishbyney skeealyn scruit ayns Gaelg ec Feaill’Eoin as goaill ayrn ayns Aundyryn Kiaull Theay Vannin as yn Guild as cur magh dy speeideilagh nyn jengey ain.  Va jees jeh nyn baitçhyn scoill moyrnagh dy hassoo ass lieh Vannin ayns Glaschu ec aundyryn Film G ayns y chied vee jeh’n niarragh.

English: We were able to promote the language during our filming of the UNESCO Biosphere award winners’ film https://youtu.be/M-yN2Iu7mLY?si=3-PeEuR3hFYorBbe
Manx: Va shin abyl dy chur er e toshiaght yn çhengey tra va shin jannoo yn fillym chossyn aundyr Biosphere UNESCO https://youtu.be/M-yN2Iu7mLY?si=3-PeEuR3hFYorBbe

English: Bunscoill Ghaelgagh teacher, Adrian Cain, coordinates Bunscoill Ghaelgagh Manx content for social media, with the 100 Laa series on Instagram having had nearly 160,000 plays so far.
Manx: Ta fer-ynsee y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh, Adrian Cain, co-oardaghey stoo ny meanyn sheshoil jeant ec y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh, as ta’n strane 100 Laa er Instagram er ve er ny chloie bunnys 160,000 keayrt choud’s shoh.

English: Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh has provided Manx lessons in schools upon request and actively promoted and supported the use of Manx within the Island’s educational institutions. Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh teachers have taken part in specialist language teacher training organised by Culture Vannin to further enhance their lesson delivery skills. Members of Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh contributed submissions to experts on Island regarding the promotion of the Manx language.
Manx: Ta’n Skimmee Gaelgagh er chiarail lessoonyn Gaelgagh ayns scoillyn tra ta’d shirit as t’ad dy breeoil cur er e hoshiaght ymmyd ny Gaelgey ayns sheshaghtyn ynsee yn Ellan. Ta fir as mraane-ynsee yn Skimmee Gaelgagh er ghoaill ayrn ayns traenal ynsee er-lheh va reaghit ec Culture Vannin dy yannoo eer ny share nyn schleiyn son gynsagh lessoonyn. Ta oltyn yn Skimmee Gaelgagh er choyrt treealtyssyn dys oaylleeyn er yn Ellan bentyn rish cur yn ghlare Vanninagh er e toshiaght.

English: The Manx Language Officer attended the Celtic Knot conference in Waterford, Ireland, and Scottish language forum in Belfast, Ireland.

MLRG has continued to organise outreach events targeting both the local and international communities. Notably, the group organised the English-medium Ned Maddrell lecture as part of the programme for the 2023 Cooish Festival.
Manx: Ta’n Possan Ronsee Gaelgagh tannaghtyn dy reaghey taghyrtyn mastey’n theay ta jeerit er chammah pobbleyn ynnydagh as eddyr-ashoonagh. Feer chronnal va leaght Ned Maddrell va er ny cummal ayns Baarle, myr ayrn jeh claare Feailley yn Chooish 2023.

English: Jeebin members delivered training to Tynwald members (Manx language training & advocacy).
Manx: Hug oltyn Jeebin traenal da oltyn yn Chiare as Feed (Traenal Gaelgagh as Shirrey Foayr yn Theay).

English: The 2023 Cooish Manx language festival took place between 1st – 5th November. It featured 20 events over 5 days, along with 8 additional workshops within one larger event. An inter-primary school postcard project, run by DESC MLU, took place, and reached around 600 children across island schools. Jeebin members worked with the wider Manx language community to organise events, and the programme was curated by Culture Vannin’s Manx Language Development Officer.
Manx: Hie Feailley Gaelgagh yn Chooish 2023 er cummal eddyr yn chied as yn wheiggoo laa jeh Mee Houney. Ghow eh stiagh 20 taghyrt harrish 5 laa, marish shen va 8 keirdlannyn elley çheu-sthie jeh un taghyrt smoo. Va shalee kaart-post ayn eddyr ny bunscoillyn, hie er stiurey liorish DESC MLU, as ren shen roshtyn mysh 600 dy chloan ayns scoillyn yn ellan. Dobbree oltyn Jeebin marish yn phobble Gaelgagh cadjin dy chummal taghyrtyn, as va’n claare er ny reaghey liorish Yn Ghreinneyder - Ben-Oik Lhiasee Ghaelgagh Culture Vannin.

English: Legislation continues to be translated into Manx and this can be found here.
Manx: Ta sleih tannaghtyn dy hyndaa slattyssyn dys Gaelg as t’ad ry-gheddyn ayns shoh.

English: Culture Vannin ran three workshops in January 2024: a Manx for worship workshop, Taize chant workshop, and Manx traditional song workshop, each lasting three hours, in January, with around 60 attendees across the three workshops. These helped to promote Manx as a valued part of our distinctive contemporary, cultural, and national identity, and reach new learners.
Manx: Ren Culture Vannin three keirdlannyn y stiurey ayns mee s’jerree yn yeuree 2024: keirdlann Gaelg cour ooashley, keirdlann arraneyn Taize, as keirdlann arraneyn tradishoonagh Manninagh, dagh unnane three ooryn er liurid, ayns mee s’jerree yn yeuree, as haink mysh 60 ooilley cooidjagh dys ny three keirdlannyn. Chooin ad shoh dy chur er e toshiaght yn Ghaelg myr ayrn scanshoil jeh nyn yarroo-enney ashoonagh, cultooroil, co-emshyragh unnaneagh, as roshtyn ynseydee noa.

English: Academic members of the group continue to publish their own research on a wide range of historical and contemporary topics of relevance to the Manx language community. Important recent contributions include Dr Erin McNulty’s PhD thesis (University of Glasgow, 2024) entitled “Cre’n sorçh dy Ghaelg t’ou loayrt nish?”: The intersection of ideologies and morphosyntactic variation in new speakers of Manx Gaelic, and her articles ‘Aavioghey as y Breear: Language Revitalization and the Manx Verbal System’. Journal of Celtic Linguistics 24 (2023): 85–124) and ‘Manx Speakers, Language, and Identity’ (Studia Celtica Posnaniensia 8 (2023): 1–24), as well as Dr Christopher Lewin’s article Lewin, Christopher (2024). ‘“It was a disaster in Ireland”: discourses around Manx as an optional or compulsory school subject’. (Linguistic Minorities in Europe, 2024). Some of these publications are of direct relevance to policy development for Manx.
Manx: Ta oltyn ard-schoillaragh jeh’n phossan tannaghtyn dy chur magh nyn ronsaght hene mychione caghlaa mooar dy chooishyn ny laghyn t’er noll shaghey as ny laghyn t’ayn nish, ta scanshoil da’n phobble Gaelgagh. Mastey reddyn jeh scansh va scruit er y gherrid ta thesis PhD Dr Erin McNulty (Ollooscoill Glaschu, 2024) enmyssit “Cre’n sorçh dy Ghaelg t’ou loayrt nish?”: The intersection of ideologies and morphosyntactic variation in new speakers of Manx Gaelic, as ny hartyn eck ‘Aavioghey as y Breear: Language Revitalization and the Manx Verbal System’. Journal of Celtic Linguistics 24 (2023): 85–124) as ‘Manx Speakers, Language, and Identity’ (Studia Celtica Posnaniensia 8 (2023): 1–24), as marish shen art voish Dr Custal Lewin, Lewin, Christopher (2024). ‘“It was a disaster in Ireland”: discourses around Manx as an optional or compulsory school subject’. (Linguistic Minorities in Europe, 2024). Ta paart jeh’n chooid scruit shoh jeh scansh as ymmyd mooar dauesyn ta lhiasaghey polaseeyn son y Ghaelg.

English: In addition, Lewin and McNulty are currently co-editing a special issue of a prominent international sociolinguistics journal about the revivals of Manx past and present. This publication is nearing completion and will showcase the voices of a number of practitioners involved on the ground in Manx language revitalization, as well as academics. It is hoped the publication will attract international attention and stimulate future research on the language.
Manx: Chammah’s shoh, ta Lewin as McNulty mysh co-femblal cur magh er-lheh mychione aavioghaghyn ny Gaelgey nish as ‘sy traa t’er noll shaghey ayns earishlioar ooasle ashoonagh mychione glareydys sheshoil. Ta’n earishlioar shoh bunnys aarloo as nee ee coraaghyn earroo dy ‘leih y hoilshaghey ta goaill ayrn ayns aavioghey ny Gaelgey mastey’n theay hene chammah’s ard-schoillaryn. Ta treisht ayn dy jean yn earishlioar shoh geill y hayrn veih feiy ny cruinney as tooilley ronsaghey y ghreinnaghey ayns y traa ry-heet.

English: The Department of Education, Sport and Culture’s Education Advisory Service aims to develop an interactive online reporting system which can be used by all members of Jeebin as a means of monitoring compliance with and application of Part III of the European Charter for Regional or Minority Languages.
Manx: Ta Shirveish Choyrlee yn Rheynn Ynsee, Spoyrt, as Cultoor kiarail dy lhiasaghey corys eddyr-obbragh er-linney cour cur coontey vees ry-gheddyn da dy chooilley olt dy Yeebin myr saase dy scrutaghey vel Ayrn III jeh Conaant Coonceil yn Oarpey son Glaraghyn Ardjynagh as Myn-ghlaraghyn goll er cur ayns bree er aght cairagh as kiart.

English: Theme 3: Manx for the Manx speaking community
Manx: Cooish 3: Gaelg son y phobble Gaelgagh

English: Strategic Commitment
Manx: Dean strateyshagh

English: We will support the social use of the Manx language at home, work, in schools and educational settings and in free time.
Manx: Nee mayd ymmyd sheshoil ny Gaelgey y phohlldal ec y thie, ec yn obbyr, ayns scoillyn as ayns traa seyr.

English: We will work to ensure the language is inclusive and reflects contemporary society.
Manx: Nee mayd gobbragh dy hickyraghey dy vel yn çhengey foshlit da dy chooilley pheiagh as dy vel ee cooie da sheshaght ny laghyn t’ayn nish.

English: We will support parents who raise their children as Manx language speakers, and those who send their children to Manx medium education, and develop opportunities for social use of the Manx language.
Manx: Nee mayd cooney lesh paarantyn ta troggal nyn gloan myr loayrtee Gaelgagh, as lhieusyn ta cur nyn gloan dy ve ynsit trooid Gaelg, as nee mayd caaghyn y chroo son ymmyd sheshoil y Ghaelg.

English: We will promote good communication between language groups both nationally and internationally.
Manx: Nee mayd cur er e hoshiaght co-haggloo eddyr possanyn glare chammah ayns Mannin as ‘sy theihll eddyr-ashoonagh.

English: Progression
Manx: Goll er e hoshiaght

English: Mooinjer Veggey’s Resources Officer is developing Activity Packs for each week of the year, with themes based upon the Early Years Foundation Stage curriculum and the Manx Cultural Calendar. The packs are supporting the increased use of Manx at the Nursery and Possan Cloie groups and are available for use by other Nurseries and groups. This is particularly important in meeting target 3.1 in the Manx Language Strategy.
Manx: Ta Fer-oik Couyryn Vooinjer Veggey lhiasaghey Paggaghyn Roortys cour gagh shiaghtin ny bleeaney, as cooishyn soit er curriglym Keim Undinagh Bleeantyn Toshee as Feaillere Cultooroil Vannin. Ta ny paggaghyn cooney lesh yn ymmyd smoo dy Ghaelg t’ayns Roïe-scoillyn as Possanyn Cloie as t’ad ry-gheddyn da Roïe-scoillyn elley as da possanyn elley. Ta shoh feer scanshoil son dy chooilleeney dean 3.1 ayns yn Strateysh son y Ghaelg.

English: Colouring and Activity Sheets have been made for both Mooinjer Veggey’s groups but also social media and community print (e.g., Green’s Cafe), for Manx Language engagement and raising awareness of Mooinjer Veggey. Original drawings for some of the main Manx Calendar customs have been produced by the previous Resources Officer and are available on the Mooinjer Veggey website for anyone to use.
Manx: Va Duillagyn Daahghey as Duillagyn Roortys er nyn yannoo son yn daa phossan t’ec Mooinjer Veggey agh chammah’s shen va duillagyn er nyn brental son ny meanyn sheshoil as yn phobble (e.g. Thie-bee Greens), dy roshtyn magh dys y theay mychione y Ghaelg as bishaghey enney jeh Mooinjer Veggey. Va jallooyn noa son paart jeh ny cliaghtaghyn Manninagh er nyn groo liorish yn Fer-oik Couyryn v’ayn roish shoh, as t’ad ry-gheddyn er ynnyd-eggey Vooinjer Veggey da peiagh erbee saillish ymmyd y yannoo jeu.

English: Bunscoill Ghaelgagh continue to welcome adult speakers as helpers within the school. They have also put on several events, including a musical of Snow White shown over two evenings in the theatre at the Centenary Centre. Culture Vannin filmed and made sound recordings of the musical for future release.
Manx: Ta’n Vunscoill Ghaelgagh tannaghtyn dy oltaghey loayrtee as fir-choonee aasit stiagh ‘sy scoill. T’ad er chummal ymmodee taghyrtyn, goaill stiagh yn cloie-kiaullee Sniaghtey Bane va er ny chloie harrish daa astyr ayns y thie-cloie ec yn Ynnyd Keead Blein. Ren Culture Vannin fillym as recortyssyn sheean y yannoo jeh’n chloie-kiaullee vees currit magh ‘sy traa ry-heet.

English: Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh have supported pupils who have wanted to use Manx in national competitions, supported staff and pupils that have wanted to use Manx in whole school assemblies, school events and displays. The Possan Gaelgagh, an enrichment club for Manx speaking young people at QE2 High School, produced an animation submitted to an international competition (FilmG) and produced newsletters in Manx. At St. Ninians Lower School, there are two clubs for learners to play games in Manx: a lunch time and afterschool club. Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh have had combined training sessions with Bunscoill Ghaelgagh staff.
Manx: Ta’n Skimmee Gaelgagh er chooney lesh paitçhyn scoill va geearree jannoo ymmyd jeh Gaelg ayns co-hirraghyn eddyr-ashoonagh, as skimmee as ynseydee  va geearree jannoo ymmyd jeh Gaelg ayns co-chruinnaghtyn yn slane scoill, as taishbynyssyn as taghyrtyn yn scoill. Ta Possan Gaelgagh, club berçhaghey cour loayrtee aegey ny Gaelgey ayns Scoill Ard BE2, er chroo screeunyn-naight ayns Gaelg. Ec Scoill S’inshley Noo Ninian, ta daa chlub ayn son ynseydee dy chloie gammanyn ayns Gaelg: club ec traa jinnairagh as club lurg scoill. Ta’n Skimmee Gaelgagh er nyannoo seshoonyn traenal marish skimmee y Vunscoill Ghaelgagh.

English: Monthly Learn Manx newsletters from Culture Vannin provide news from across the Manx language community. Culture Vannin encourages people to contact Yn Greinneyder, the Manx Language Development Officer, to share news. An archive of past newsletters can be found at: www.learnmanx.com/newsletters
Manx: Ta screeunyn-naight meeagh veih Culture Vannin cur naight veih fud yn phobble Gaelgagh. Ta Culture Vannin greinnaghey sleih dy loayrt rish yn Ghreinneyder, yn Ven-Oik Lhiasee Ghaelgagh, dy rheynn naight. Ta tashtey-recortys jeh screeunyn-naight chaie ry-gheddyn ayns shoh:  www.learnmanx.com/newsletters

English: Social media channels provide ongoing support, promotion and use of Manx, across accounts such as Culture Vannin, Learn Manx, Bunscoill Ghaelgagh, Mooinjer Veggey and Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh.
Manx: Ta ammyryn meanyn sheshoil cur cooney kinjagh, cur y Ghaelg er e toshiaght as cur boayl ayn oddys ymmyd ve jeant jeh’n Ghaelg, ammyryn lheid as Culture Vannin, Ynsee Gaelg, Bunscoill Ghaelgagh, Mooinjer Veggey as Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh.

English: Culture Vannin offers ten weekly Manx classes for adults, resulting in just under 5,500 instances of participation each year. Two classes coincide with school drop-off times, and seven classes take place in the evenings or at weekends, to provide those who are restricted by normal working hours a chance to participate. Four classes take place via Zoom and six classes take place in person. Culture Vannin classes reach a wide age group, from those in their twenties through to students in their eighties and promotes intergenerational exchanges. Thanks to the inclusion of online classes, the diaspora of Manx speakers is becoming ever wider.
Manx: Ta Culture Vannin çhebbal jeih brastyllyn shiaghtinoil cour sleih aasit, yn eiyrtys jeh shoh, ta shen dy vel sleih goaill ayrn bunnys 5,500 keayrt gagh blein. Ta daa vrastyl taghyrt ec yn traa ta paarantyn cur lhieu nyn gloan dys ny veih’n scoill, as ta shiaght brastyllyn taghyrt ayns yn ‘astyr beg ny ec y jerrey-shiaghtin, dy chur caa dauesyn t’er nyn lhiettal liorish ooryn obbyr cadjyn dy ghoaill ayrn. Ta kiare brastyllyn goaill ynnyd er Zoom as ta shey brastyllyn goaill ynnyd ayns-persoon. Ta brastyllyn Culture Vannin roshtyn sleih jeh caghlaaghyn eash, vouesyn ta mysh feed blein dy eash dys studeyryn as yn kiare-feed ny yei oc, as t’eh cur er e hoshiaght coloayrtys eddyr sheelogheyn.  Bwooise da’n aght ta brastyllyn er-linney goaill stiagh dy chooilley pheiagh, ta’n diaspora Gaelgagh gaase eer ny smoo.

English: Culture Vannin supports Manx Gaelic broadcasting through its work with the Gaelic Broadcasting Committee, and through disseminating films created in, or about, Manx. Films can be found on the Culture Vannin YouTube channels and have a wide reach, with 4,710,547 total video views as of end of March 2024.
Manx: Ta Culture Vannin pohlldal ymskeaylley Gaelgagh lesh nyn obbyr marish Bing Ymskeaylley Gaelgagh, as trooid skeaylley magh filmyn hie er croo ayns, ny mychione, Gaelg. Ta filmyn ry-gheddyn er ammyryn Youtube Culture Vannin as ta roshtyn vooar oc, as hie ny feeshanyn er fakin 4,710,547 dy cheayrtyn ec jerrey mee veanagh y niarree 2024.

English: Culture Vannin organised and ran a teacher-training day with Helen Prosser, Director of Teaching and Learning for Dysgu Cymraeg (Learn Welsh) in March 2024. Two sessions took place; one for confident Manx speakers who would like to teach in the community, and another for established teachers. These sessions were well attended, and feedback was excellent, with participants gaining more confidence, ideas, and a greater understanding of methodology.
Manx: Ren Culture Vannin laa traenal cour fir-ynsee y reaghey as y stiurey marish Helen Prosser, Stiureyder Fir-ynsee as Ynseydee da Dysgu Cymraeg (gynsagh Bretnish) ayns mee veanagh y niarree 2024. Ghow daa heshoon ynnyd; yn derrey yeh son loayrtee Gaelgagh shickyr bailleu ve nyn vir-ynsee Gaelgagh, as yn jeh elley son fir-ynsee t’ayn hannah. Haink palçhey dy ‘leih dys ny seshoonyn shoh, as mie er bashtal va’n aaveaghey, as yn sleih ghow ayrn gaase ayns shickyrys, eieyn, as toiggalys jeh’n taase-oaylleeaght.

English: Yn Greinneyder, the Manx Language Development Officer for Culture Vannin, contributed to a minority languages conference in January 2024 at Japan House, London. The conference also featured round-table discussions, led by Dr. Julia Sallabank from SOAS, University of London.
Manx: Ghow yn Greinneyder, Ben-Oik Lhiasee Ghaelgagh da Culture Vannin ayrn ayns co-haggloo son myn-ghlaraghyn ayns mee s’jerree yn yeuree 2024 ec Thie Japan, Lunnin. Va resoonaghtyn boayrd cruinn ec yn cho-haggloo neesht, er nyn leeideil liorish Dr. Julia Sallabank veih SOAS, Ollooscoill Lunnin.

English: Future Work
Manx: Obbyr ‘sy Traa Ry-Heet

English: Possan Cloie has been highly successful in recruiting new interest in Manx learning for families, nursery and ultimately the Bunscoill Ghaelgagh. Mooinjer Veggey will continue to seek opportunities to expand their Possan Cloie network. There are three Possan Cloie groups now but funding all of these remains a significant problem.
Manx: Ta Possan Cloie er ve speeideilagh erskyn towse as ad prowal dy vishaghey sym er gynsagh Gaelg mastey lughtyn-thie, roïe-scoillyn as myr eiyrtys lurg shen yn Vunscoill Ghaelgagh. Tannee Mooinjer Veggey dy hirrey caaghyn dy vooadaghey nyn moggyl dy Phossanyn Cloie. Ta three Possanyn Cloie ayn nish agh ta geddyn argid son ny possanyn shoh dy kinjagh ny ard-voirey.

English: Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh hope to forge links with other peripatetic language teams and minority language professionals.  Some members of the team hope to attend and contribute to British Irish Council webinars and research.
Manx: Ta’n Skimmee Gaelgagh treishteil dy jean ad kianglaghyn y chroo rish skimmeeyn rouailtagh elley as sleih profeshoonagh ta gobbragh lesh myn-ghlaraghyn. Ta paart dy oltyn y skimmee jerkal rish taaghey as goaill ayrn ayns webinaryn as ronsaght y Choonceil Ghoaldagh-Yernagh.

English: Theme 4: Lifelong learning of the Manx language from early to later years
Manx: Cooish 4: Ynsagh feiy’n vea voish oikanys dys shenn-eash

English: Strategic Commitment
Manx: Dean Strateyshagh

English: We will ensure that anyone, of any age, anywhere, has the opportunity to learn and speak the Manx language and that the language’s role in community cohesion is recognised.
Manx: Nee mayd shickyraghey dy bee ec peiagh erbee, boayl erbee, caa dy ynsagh as dy loayrt y Ghaelg as dy bee scansh y ghlare son cooidjaghtys y phobble er ny toiggal.

English: We will support the development of a clear pathway and training programme for the recruitment and retention of language practitioners in educational settings.
Manx: Nee mayd cooney lesh croo cassan ynsee as claare traenal shickyr son geddyn as freayll obbreeyn Gaelgagh ayns buill ynsee.

English: We will work to increase the numbers of speakers and learners of Manx to 5,000 by 2032.
Manx: Nee mayd gobbragh dy vishaghey yn earroo dy loayrtee as ynseydee yn Ghaelg dys 5,000 roish 2032.

English: We will explore ways to grow provision for the language.
Manx: Nee mayd shirrey magh aghtyn dy vishaghey mooadys y chooney ta kiarit da’n ghlare.

English: Progression
Manx: Goll er e hoshiaght

English: Each of Mooinjer Veggey’s Possan Cloie groups brings about twelve new families to the Manx language each year. If this growth continues at the same pace Mooinjer Veggey would expect to see several hundred new supporters and speakers of Manx by the end of the 2022-2032 Manx Language Strategy.
Manx: Ta dagh unnane jeh Possanyn Cloie Vooinjer Veggey cur lesh stiagh mysh ghaa-yeig dy lughtyn-thie noa dys y Ghaelg dagh blein. My vees yn aase shoh goll er ec y vieauid cheddin, bee Mooinjer Veggey jerkal rish fakin keead ny ghaa dy ‘leih ta cummal seose y Ghaelg as sleih as Gaelg oc roish jerrey yn Strateysh son y Ghaelg 2022-2032.

English: Mooinjer Veggey have had a successful year in fund raising events, though they continue to rely on considerable voluntary effort to ensure the organisation can continue to support preschool, primary and parental learning.
Manx: Ta blein feer speeideilagh er ve Mooinjer Veggey bentyn rish taghyrtyn son troggal argid, ga dy vel ad tannaghtyn dy lhie er obbyr dy liooar jeant liorish arryltee dy hickyraghey dy vod yn çheshaght tannaghtyn dy chummal seose ynsagh ayns roie-scoillyn, bunscoillyn as son paarantyn.

English: Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh have trialled some lessons where KS4 Manx language learners have listened in to Manx language immersion lessons at QE2. The Manx Language Officer has commenced work on a schools’ Manx grammar book. Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh have attended high school open and option evenings talking about potential Manx language learning pathways and lifelong learning to parents and students.
Manx: Ta’n Skimmee Gaelgagh er phrowal paart dy lessoonyn raad ta ynseydee ec Ard-Cheim 4 çheet stiagh as geaishtagh rish lessoonyn jeant trooid Gaelg ec BE2. Ta’n Oaseir Gaelgagh er ghoaill toshiaght er lioar ghrammar Gaelgagh son scoillyn. Ta’n Skimmee Gaelgagh er daaghey fastyryn foshlit as fastyryn reih ec ardscoillyn dy loayrt mychione cassanyn ynsee Gaelgagh possibyl as ynsagh beayn da paarantyn as studeyryn.

English: As detailed earlier in the report, the Manx Corpus supports the learning and use of Manx across numerous sectors of the community, and MLRG is contributing to policy discussions around the further development of the Manx, both directly and via academic publications by its members.
Manx: Myr va imraait dy myn roïe ayns y tuarastyl shoh, ta’n Corpus Gaelgagh cummal seose ynsagh as ymmyd ny Gaelgey tessyn ymmodee rheynnyn yn phobble, as ta’n PRG goaill ayrn ayns resoonaghtyn polasee mychione lhiasaghey yn Ghaelg ny sodjey, chammah dy jeeragh as trooid pabyryn ardschoillaragh currit magh liorish oltyn yn phossan.

English: DESC Education Advisory Service has developed Manx Language Charter for Schools – ‘Gaelg Campus’ based on the Welsh Language Charter. The charter is based on three levels Bronze, Silver, Gold aiming to ensure that a school follows challenging but attainable targets in connection with promoting the use of the Manx Language. Schools who achieve the Gold Award will be invited to act as a hub school for other schools wishing to embark on following the charter.
Manx: Ta’n Çhirveish-Choyrlee Ynsee er lhiasaghey Conaant Gaelgagh cour Scoillyn - ‘Gaelg Campus’ ta soit er conaant yn Ghlare Vretnagh. Ta’n conaant soit er three keimyn Ooha, Argid, Airh, ta kiarail shickyraghey dy vel scoill geiyrt er deanyn ta doillee agh roshtynagh ta bentyn rish jannoo ymmyd jeh’n Ghaelg. Scoillyn ta cooilleeney yn Aundyr Airh, bee ad er nyn guirrey dy ve nyn scoill-hub son scoillyn elley ta geearree cummal seose yn conaant.

English: Culture Vannin runs ten weekly Manx lessons for adults, which are clearly progressive and levelled, and available at a range of times, both in-person and online. The lessons are supported by a four-part course, called Loayr Gaelg, accompanied by 200 sound files online. Yn Greinneyder has developed a large number of resources to support learners, meaning that retention of learners, and annual feedback received from them, is excellent.
Manx: Ta Culture Vannin stiurey jeih brastyllyn shiaghtinoil da sleih aasit, ta’n coorse er ny rheynn ayns keimyn as ta immeeaght leayr veih’n derrey cheim dys y keim elley. Ta ny brastyllyn ry-gheddyn ec caghlaaghyn traa, chammah ayns-persoon as er-linney. Ta ny lessoonyn soit er coorse ayns kiare ayrnyn, enmyssit Loayr Gaelg, as ta 200 coadan sheean er-linney goll marish. Ta’n Greinneyder er chroo ymmodee couyryn dy chooney lesh ynseydee, yn eiyrtys jeh shoh, ta shen dy vel ny hynseydee er nyn vreayll, as ta aaveaghey bleinoil voue mie er bashtal.

English: Future Work
Manx: Obbyr ‘sy Traa Ry-Heet

English: Mooinjer Veggey plans to open a further two Possan Cloie groups as resource allows. As outlined above this would significantly contribute to new speakers and supporters of Manx by 2032.
Manx: Ta Mooinjer Veggey plannal dy ‘osley daa Phossan Cloie elley my vees argid as ynnydyn  dy liooar ayn. Myr ta imraait heose, veagh shoh bishaghey yn earroo dy loayrtee noa as sleih ta cummal seose yn Ghaelg roish 2032.

English: Mooinjer Veggey continues to look for new fund-raising opportunities to allow the charity to expand its work and influence.
Manx: Ta Mooinjer Veggey tannaghtyn dy hirrey magh caaghyn elley dy hroggal argid vees lhiggey da’n çheshaght yiastyllagh e hobbyr as e bree y vooadaghey.

English: Yn Skimmee Gaelgagh will work to identify gaps in provision of lifelong learning of Manx and to further develop options for students that wish to learn Manx to identify clearer progression pathways. Possibly liaising with other agencies, they hope to create bitesize work-based experience opportunities for learners who would like to work towards speaking Manx within an educational work environment.
Manx: Obbree yn Skimmee Gaelgagh dy chur enney er baarnaghyn ayns kiarail son ynsagh beayn ny Gaelgey as nee ad reihyn y lhiasaghey ny sodjey son studeyryn ta geearree Gaelg y ynsagh, dy gheddyn cassanyn immeeaght s’baghtal.

English: The MLRG and its members will continue to contribute to discussions on the development of lifelong learning opportunities and resources.
Manx: Nee yn PRG as e oltyn tannaghtyn dy ghoaill ayrn ayns resoonaghtyn mychione lhiasaghey caaghyn as couyryn cour ynsagh beayn.

English: MLRG further plans to launch a survey of Manx language organisations within the coming year and release a report and recommendations which it is hoped will inform discussions around the 5,000-speaker target, training, and recruitment within education at all levels, and how and where provision can be grown.
Manx: Ny sodjey, ta’n PRG kiarail creear jeh sheshaghtyn Gaelgagh y lunney çheu-sthie jeh’n vlein shoh çheet as tuarystal as saaseyn moyllit y chur magh vees cur fys da resoonaghtyn bentyn rish dean ny 5,000 loayrtagh, traenal, as tayrn stiagh sleih noa ayns ynsagh ec dy chooilley cheim, as kys as c’raad oddys kiarail ve er ny aase.

English: Conclusion
Manx: Jerrey

English: This year shows good progress towards the aims of the Strategy. In particular, it shows that working together across agencies has a greater impact for the language and the language community. Resourcing issues still exist – both in terms of personnel and funding – and the language community recognises the importance of its grassroots and their voluntary contribution. We need to find innovative ways to encourage people to work for the language, and to have the confidence to do so.
Manx: Myleeaney ta shin er jeet er nyn doshiaght dy mie bentyn rish cooilleeney deanyn yn Strateysh. Er-lheh, t’eh soilshaghey dy vel co-obbraghey mastey sheshaghtyn ny haase mie dy vishaghey yn Ghaelg as yn pobble Gaelgagh. Ta boiraghyn foast ayn - bentyn rish chammah geddyn skimmee as argid - as ta pobble yn ghlare cur enney er yn scansh jeh obbyr arryltagh yn cho-phobble. Shegin dooin saaseyn noa-chliaghtagh y gheddyn dy ghreinnaghey sleih dy obbragh son yn ghlare, as dy ennaghtyn shickyr dy liooar dy yannoo shen.

English: The report shows the balance in sustaining activity and extending it, for example through the production of new resources. A great deal of Manx language material is now available freely online, which supports teaching in nurseries, schools, and the adult language learning community. Training for teachers has been important in extending their already excellent contribution, and support for parents is also crucial. We know that new research will contribute greatly to policy development.
Manx: Ta’n tuarystal soilshaghey yn cormid t’ayn ayns cummal seose shen ny ta goll er jannoo as eh y vooadaghey, myr sampleyr trooid croo couyryn noa. Ta mooarane stoo Gaelgagh ry-gheddyn dy nastee nish er-linney, as she shoh cooney mooar da fir as mraane-ynsee ayns roïe-scoillyn, scoillyn as da pobble yn sleih aasit ta gynsagh yn ghlare. Ta traenal son fir as mraane-ynsee er ve scanshoil son dy vel eh mooadaghey yn obbyr yindyssagh t’ad jannoo hannah, as ta cooney da paarantyn scanshoil erskyn towse neesht. Ta fys ain dy jean ronsaght noa cooney lesh lhiasaghey polaseeyn dy mooar.

English: Jeebin looks forward to preparations for the Year of the Manx Language in 2026. The year-long celebration of the language will help raise its profile, encourage wider support and engagement, and further build towards the ambitious target of 5,000 Manx speakers by 2032.
Manx: Ta Jeebin jeeaghyn rhymboo lesh jannoo aarloo cour Blein ny Gaelgey ayns 2026. Nee yn ard-eailley blein er lhiurid jeh’n ghlare cooney lesh bishaghey’n goo eck, greinaghey cooney veih ny smoo dy ‘leih as tayrn sleih stiagh. Nee eh cooney lesh roshtyn yn dean gloyrviandagh, ta shen dy ghra 5,000 dy loayrtee Gaelgagh liorish 2032.
